title: Change the path of script-exporter-script (move executable out of /etc/)
summary: The user-supplied script is written as an executable to /etc/script-exporter-script (src/charm.py:52), but /etc is for configuration, not executables; the file is written with mode=0o755 (src/charm.py:165), confirming it is an executable. The issue asks to relocate it to a more appropriate location such as /opt/ or /usr/bin/ (or under the charm's own dir). The path is referenced only via the single attribute self._single_script_path, so the change is centralized.
why_good_first_issue: Labelled 'good first issue'. Single constant to change (src/charm.py:52) plus verifying the handful of internal references that all go through self._single_script_path. Clear definition of done; no architecture knowledge needed; unit tests do not hardcode the string so risk is low.
affected_files: src/charm.py:52 (self._single_script_path = LocalPath("/etc/script-exporter-script")); referenced at src/charm.py:112 (cleanup), :165 (write_text mode=0o755), :200 and :204 (script-name handling), :233 (scrape job target)
repro_steps: grep -rn '/etc/script-exporter-script' and '_single_script_path' src/charm.py. Confirm the path is an executable via the write mode at src/charm.py:165.
repro_result: Confirmed in code (current HEAD): src/charm.py:52 defines self._single_script_path = LocalPath("/etc/script-exporter-script"); src/charm.py:165 writes it with mode=0o755 (i.e. an executable placed directly in /etc). Expected-after-fix: the script lives under an appropriate executables/state location (e.g. /opt/script-exporter/ or /usr/local/bin/), not /etc. Baseline unit suite: 4 passed (tox -e unit). Tests do not reference the literal path, so they remain green.
fix_hint: Change the constant at src/charm.py:52 to a non-/etc location (e.g. LocalPath("/opt/script-exporter/script") or under self._script_exporter_dir but clearly separated). All usages go through self._single_script_path so they follow automatically; double-check _remove_file_dir cleanup (:112) and the scrape-job path (:233). Update any docs/config defaults if they mention the old path.
notes: Machine (systemd) charm, not k8s. Issue open & valid; labels include 'good first issue', Type: Enhancement, Status: Triage. ops main entrypoint here already uses the modern form (ops.main(...) per traceback in issue #16), so no ops.main cleanup needed.

title: Failed systemd unit results in charm getting stuck in config-changed hook
summary: If a bad config_file makes the systemd service fail to start, the next config-changed (even one that fixes the config) crashes because _create_systemd_service calls service_restart then service_resume on a service that is not running, and service_resume raises SystemdError uncaught. This wedges the unit: the user cannot recover by fixing the config. The charm also restarts the service in multiple paths (src/charm.py:122, :301, :304) without guarding for a service that fails to come up with invalid config.
why_good_first_issue: Clear, reproducible bug with a precise traceback pointing at src/charm.py:_create_systemd_service (service_resume). Fix is localized (guard/ordering of service start vs config write, or catch SystemdError and surface BlockedStatus). Slightly above trivial due to systemd interaction, so a good intermediate first issue.
affected_files: src/charm.py:278-304 (_create_systemd_service: daemon_reload + service_restart + service_resume); src/charm.py:114-124 (_on_config_changed restarts service); src/charm.py:149-155 (_set_config_file writes config then creates service)
repro_steps: Repro plan (needs live deploy on a machine model): 1) juju deploy script-exporter (latest/edge) with a working config_file. 2) juju config config_file=<broken yaml/command> so the systemd unit fails to start. 3) juju config config_file=<valid again>. Observe: config-changed raises charms.operator_libs_linux.v1.systemd.SystemdError: Attempted to resume 'script-exporter.service', but it is not running (per issue traceback), leaving the unit in error and unrecoverable via config.
repro_result: LIVE-REPRODUCED 2026-06-16 (lxd: ubuntu+script-exporter dev/edge): malformed config_file -> unit stuck 'hook failed config-changed / awaiting error resolution'; unguarded yaml.safe_load at charm.py:186 (a 2nd unguarded point besides service_resume). || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Code-level confirmation: src/charm.py:304 calls service_resume(SERVICE_FILENAME) unconditionally after service_restart at :301; if the service failed to start (bad config), service_resume raises SystemdError which propagates out of the config-changed handler. Cannot reproduce at unit tier because it depends on a real systemd refusing to start the unit; the unit tests stub systemd.
fix_hint: In _create_systemd_service, write config/unit file first, then start the service defensively: wrap service_restart/service_resume in try/except SystemdError and, on failure, append BlockedStatus('script-exporter failed to start, check config') instead of letting the hook crash. Ensure a subsequent valid config can still reload+restart. Optionally use service_enable + service_start instead of resume.
notes: Machine (systemd) charm. Issue open & valid (Type: Bug, Status: Triage). Baseline unit suite passes (4 passed). Flagged integration because the failure requires a real failed systemd unit.
